title: init / switch -c bootstrap leaves the substrate config-less, with no op-driven path to a custom-vocabulary config
description: initProject (the context-init op) and switchAndCreate (/context switch -c) scaffold the substrate dir + schemas/ subdir but write no config.json. The only op that originates a config is acceptCanonicalConfig/accept-all, which adopts the entire packaged conception (all-or-nothing, never-clobber). amendConfigEntry throws 'no config.json' when the config is absent, so it cannot bootstrap one. Net: a user who wants a substrate with a custom vocabulary rather than the full packaged catalog has no op-driven path and must hand-author config.json, falling off the tool surface.

evidence.reference: initProject scaffolds dirs + pointer only; writes no config.json
switchAndCreate flips pointer + scaffolds dirs only; writes no config.json
amendConfigEntry throws 'no config.json' when config absent — cannot originate a config
adoptConception never-clobber guard if(existing) + whole-catalog adoption — only config-originating op, all-or-nothing
Live dogfooding state: pointer switched to using-pi-context/, which holds only an empty schemas/ subdir and no config.json — the broken state with no ops path forward

impact: Custom-vocabulary substrate creation is impossible through the tool surface; a dogfooded /context switch -c lands the user on a config-less substrate that no op can advance (accept-all would impose the whole catalog; amend-config refuses without a config).
proposed_resolution: init + switch -c write a minimal schema-valid skeleton config (schema_version + empty block_kinds + minted substrate_id) via a shared never-clobber writeSkeletonConfig helper; accept-all becomes skeleton-aware (overwrite when absent OR isSkeletonConfig, never-clobber a populated config); deriveBootstrapState gains a 'skeleton' state ordered no-pointer -> no-config -> skeleton -> not-installed -> ready; onward-guidance messages name the two paths (accept-all OR amend/edit).
